## Problem and scope

<!-- One concept. Link the issue and state owned files plus explicit exclusions. -->

## Product boundary and compatibility

<!-- Supported/experimental/internal/planned; public contracts or migrations affected. -->

## Evidence

<!-- Exact focused and required conformance commands with results. Do not include secrets. -->

## Risk and rollback

<!-- Blast radius, degraded states, and how to reverse safely. -->

## Checklist

- [ ] I used no private data, credentials, proprietary fixtures, or private-extension code.
- [ ] I updated documentation for changed public behavior and did not overstate maturity.
- [ ] I ran focused checks, relevant conformance checks, Ruff for changed Python, and `git diff --check`.
- [ ] If roadmap-related, I linked or updated the public issue/Project item.
- [ ] Security-sensitive details were reported privately under `SECURITY.md`.
